title: The God of Small Things
author: Arundhati Roy
description: A Booker Prize-winning novel set in Kerala, exploring forbidden love and the layered complexities of caste society.

title: Midnight's Children
author: Salman Rushdie
description: A sweeping epic of India's independence, narrated by a child born at the stroke of midnight with extraordinary powers.

title: A Suitable Boy
author: Vikram Seth
description: One of the longest novels in English, a tender and humorous portrayal of post-independence India searching for love and identity.

title: The White Tiger
author: Aravind Adiga
description: A darkly comic Booker Prize winner that exposes the brutal underbelly of India's new economy through the eyes of an ambitious driver.

title: The Remains of the Day
author: Kazuo Ishiguro
description: A deeply moving meditation on dignity, regret, and the quiet sacrifices of a lifetime of dedicated service.

title: The Inheritance of Loss
author: Kiran Desai
description: A Booker Prize-winning novel set in the Himalayas, exploring postcolonial loss, immigration, and longing across generations.

title: Pride and Prejudice
author: Jane Austen
description: Austen's beloved comedy of manners follows the Bennet sisters as they navigate love, class, and the marriage market in Regency England.

author: George Orwell
description: Orwell's terrifying vision of a totalitarian future where Big Brother watches every move and history is endlessly rewritten.

title: To Kill a Mockingbird
author: Harper Lee
description: Harper Lee's Pulitzer-winning masterpiece about racial injustice and moral growth in a small Alabama town, seen through a child's eyes.

title: Crime and Punishment
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
description: A psychological study of guilt, morality, and redemption, following a troubled student who commits a murder and must live with the consequences.

title: Anna Karenina
author: Leo Tolstoy
description: Tolstoy's sweeping portrait of high society Russia, following a tragic love affair against the backdrop of a changing world.

title: Jane Eyre
author: Charlotte Brontë
description: A fiercely independent governess navigates love, betrayal, and self-discovery in this landmark of English literature.

title: Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind
author: Yuval Noah Harari
description: A sweeping narrative of human history from the Stone Age through the 21st century, challenging how we think about ourselves.

title: Being Mortal
author: Atul Gawande
description: A compassionate and clear-eyed exploration of how medicine can better support the elderly and dying, written by a practicing surgeon.

title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
author: Daniel Kahneman
description: Nobel laureate Kahneman reveals the two systems that drive the way we think and explains how our minds are prone to bias.

title: The Diary of a Young Girl
author: Anne Frank
description: Anne Frank's astonishing wartime diary, written while in hiding from the Nazis — one of the most powerful accounts of the Holocaust.

title: When Breath Becomes Air
author: Paul Kalanithi
description: A neurosurgeon's memoir written as he faced terminal lung cancer — a devastating and life-affirming meditation on mortality and meaning.

title: The Great Gatsby (First Edition Facsimile)
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
description: A museum-quality facsimile reproduction of the 1925 first edition, preserving the original typography and cover art of Fitzgerald's masterpiece.

title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes (1892)
author: Arthur Conan Doyle
description: An original 1892 edition of Conan Doyle's first short story collection featuring Sherlock Holmes — a prized collector's item in fine condition.

title: Gitanjali (Signed Edition)
author: Rabindranath Tagore
description: An early signed edition of Tagore's Nobel Prize-winning poetry collection — a rare and treasured piece of Indian literary history.

title: Don Quixote (Illustrated, 1900)
author: Miguel de Cervantes
description: A beautifully illustrated early 20th-century edition with hand-drawn plates — a collector's dream and a cornerstone of world literature.

title: The Shadow Lines
author: Amitav Ghosh
description: A luminous novel about memory, belonging, and the invisible borders that divide nations and families across generations.
